name: Vietnamese Spring Rolls
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons Coriander or parsley, fresh, chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut chicken into strips measuring 1-1/2 inches by 1/2 inch.
2. In a bowl, blend soy sauce and hoisin sauces, water, orange juice concentrate, peanut butter, chili paste, and sesame oil until smooth. Stir in cornstarch. Add chicken and stir to coat; marinate for 15 minutes.
3. Cover mushrooms with warm water and soak for 15 to 50 minutes or until softened. Drain and rinse, then discard stems and slice caps into thin strips.
4. Break noodles into pieces measuring 1-1/2 inches long. Place in a bowl and cover with water; soak for 5 minutes. Then drain. In a saucepan of boiling water, cook noodles for 5 minutes. Drain and rinse with cold water; drain well.
5. Heat peanut oil over medium-high heat in a wok or skillet. Cook ginger, garlic, and scallions for 30 seconds. Add red pepper and mushrooms; cook for 3 minutes. Add chicken with marinade; cook for 3 to 4 minutes or until chicken is no longer pink inside. Stir in noodles and coriander; let cool.
6. Dip rice papers one at a time into warm water to soften, then lay out on damp tea towels in a single layer. Place about 2 tablespoons filling in the center of each triangular sheet (or 1/4 cup in each round sheet). Fold up the bottom over the filling, fold sides over and roll up tightly. Rolls can be covered with damp tea towels in separate layers and refrigerated for up to 8 hours.
7. Heat oil over medium-high heat to 375°F. Fry rolls in batches for about 5 minutes or until crisp and golden on all sides. Drain on a rack. Makes 16 large or 30 small rolls.
Instead of deep-frying, steam spring rolls in greased steamer for 5 minutes, or bake for 20 minutes in a 400-degree oven. Assembled spring rolls can also be eaten without being cooked.
